text: Thanks, Andrew for a great weekly. It was great to be at the GM and for everyone to recognise the strong foundations that have been built this year - foundations that are continuing to deepen which will help provide the base for the next stage of the 10 year plan. I appreciate all the hard work you and Jesse are doing in the background - a lot of which many can’t see or you’re unable to share but are crucial to helping improve SWC’s long term sustainability. We have 2664 Bitcoin (an incredible achievement) and Bitcoin will do a lot of the “heavy lifting” for us - this business is cyclical and it’s only a matter of time before sentiment returns - when it does SWC will be in an even stronger position to continue delivering value to shareholders.

engagement.note: These figures were correct as of May 14, 2026, 9:00 AM London time

text: It was fantastic to meet so many people at the AGM yesterday. What stood out most was that, despite the recent pullback in markets, the sector, and the share price, confidence remains strong. There is clearly a committed core of long-term holders who believe in the mission and the 10-year plan. The event itself was exceptionally well run and welcoming (thank you Laura). Attendees had time to connect, tour the aerospace museum and Concorde, and soak in an atmosphere that quietly reminded everyone of what bold engineering and vision can achieve. Andrew delivered an excellent, up-to-date presentation, followed by an informative Q&A and an informal lunch organised by the community. It was inspiring to hear the personal stories of how people discovered SWC and how it has influenced their personal and professional lives. Most importantly, all key resolutions were passed, enabling SWC to continue building out the “tools in the toolbox” that give the company both tactical flexibility (short-term options) and strategic optionality (medium- to long-term avenues) for creating shareholder value through multiple pathways. A great deal has been achieved and learned in just seven months, and yesterday offered a valuable moment to reflect on that progress. I left feeling more motivated than ever to contribute where I can, and I sensed that many others felt the same. We’re on a mission; to help orange-pill the UK and build one of the country’s largest and most impactful companies - I genuinely believe we’re capable of it.
